meta.setting: A pine switchback above French Gulch
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in road scouting and open-world travel

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Clear Creek travel, mining communities, and road pressure.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Northern California mining-town roads, stage movement, commerce, and material setting.

image_prompt.subject: a road scout on a tired horse pausing above a switchback to study wagon tracks, fresh smoke, and a distant bridge before the freight team arrives
image_prompt.scene: clear cold morning on a steep pine road; loose gravel, old rain ruts, a circling buzzard high above, town smoke far below, and a narrow road curling out of sight behind shale
image_prompt.composition: square vista with horse and rider offset left, switchback road curling like a question mark through the frame, and small evidence details visible in the foreground dust
image_prompt.camera: over-the-shoulder horseback view with early-HD open-world depth, readable trail signs, and a route-choice feel
material_cues: iron horseshoe edge at frame bottom
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Give the scout a stillness that says the worst danger is the thing not moving. The image should make the next bend feel like a decision, not a backdrop.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine travel vista
render_traits: slightly simplified horse and rider silhouette
modest texture resolution on dust, pine, leather, and shale
hard ambient occlusion under horse legs, saddle, rocks, and ruts
believable 1899 saddle gear, clothing, and road conditions
environmental storytelling through tracks, smoke, grade, and posture
style_modifiers.palette: cold blue morning, pine green, dusty tan, shale gray, worn leather brown, smoke gray, and pale sun.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable signs, labels, or map text. Route information must come from landscape and tracks.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The scout, horse, switchback, tracks, and distant route clues are fully readable.
Every visible prop and garment belongs plausibly to 1899 frontier travel.
The image suggests route choice and danger without modern UI.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
